title: poa hydrophila
intro: Poa hydrophila, also known as water meadow grass or water-loving meadow grass, is a type of flowering plant native to New Zealand. It has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as relieving digestive issues and reducing fever, and is often used to treat respiratory problems like bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, Poa hydrophila can be made into a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in poultices and compresses to treat skin conditions and wounds.
